content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "Significant shift is visible in the client's own language across the conversation. She moves from a flat, defended opening ('I don't really care, honestly') to naming previously unspoken material: the 'not clean' stuff (turn 9), the thirty years of being the one her mother called (turn 11), the relief that her mother can't call anymore (turn 11), and finally — most tellingly — the quiet self-disclosure 'I haven't said that out loud before. Any of it' and 'I think it's gotten a little away from me, actually. The drinking. I didn't used to need a whole bottle' (turn 15). That last admission, described as 'like she didn't mean to say that,' is a clear somatic/defensive softening — the client herself names the drinking as a problem rather than the coach having to. The breakthrough territory (kinked emotions of relief/anger, the 'not clean' grief, and the drinking acknowledged honestly) was traversed by the client, not just by the coach. The conversation did not fully land on referral to a grief therapist, so it is not a perfect breakthrough, but the depth criterion — a layer beneath the surface presentation surfaced and was felt — is clearly met.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: flat, defended, 'I don't really care.' Drinking framed as fine."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: 'didn't cry. Still haven't really.' Voice drops, acknowledges abnormality, then deflects."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client names 'not clean' stuff and fear of not stopping if she opens — a deeper layer surfaces, though she immediately baits with body-wisdom question."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major disclosure: thirty years of caretaking, relief that mother can't call, 'terrible person' verdict. The kinked emotion territory enters."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client softens slightly ('something shifts slightly'), names 'bad daughter' fear explicitly, then asks directly about the drinking — engaging rather than only defending."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clearest shift: 'Maybe that I'm human.' 'I haven't said that out loud before.' And the near-involuntary admission that the drinking has 'gotten a little away from me' — client herself breaks the defense."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "kinked-emotions", "What is the grief actually carrying underneath the drinking?", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "What would it be like to feel the grief without the drinking?", "Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?"],
  "notes": "Client did meaningful work; the referral-to-therapist piece of the breakthrough condition was not yet delivered in this excerpt, but the depth/shift criterion is clearly satisfied by client text. If referral never comes in later turns, that would affect Fidelity, not Depth."
}
